四维空间感知实验室
Vol. II  ·  2025 Perspectives on Civilization & Machine Intelligence APA 7th Edition Peer Commentary Series
Political Economy of Space · 空间的政治经济学

废墟经济学：注意力资本如何清空实体空间

The Economics of Ruins: How Attention Capital Hollows Physical Space
Dai Shiqi  ·  School of Architecture, Tsinghua University
M.Arch in Architecture and Urban Design  ·  Urban Spatial Intelligence
Submitted: April 2025  ·  Revised: April 2025
摘要 Abstract
本文以谷歌圣何塞 Downtown West 项目的承诺与废弃为切入点，对照伦敦国王十字的私有化公共空间实践，构建一个跨越中国（上海、天津、青岛、广州）与全球（底特律、伊斯坦布尔、孟买、首尔、贝鲁特）的历史谱系，论证注意力经济对实体城市空间的系统性侵蚀。文章援引 Harvey（2003）的"剥夺性积累"理论与 Zuboff（2019）的监控资本主义框架，主张数字资本的兴起并非空间的解放，而是一种新形式的空间掠夺。
Attention Economy POPS London Downtown West Platform Urbanism Accumulation by Dispossession Global Urban Decay Spatial Justice
Abstract
Beginning from Google's promise and abandonment of Downtown West in San José, and setting it against London King's Cross's institutionalised privately owned public space (POPS), this article constructs a typology spanning China (Shanghai, Tianjin, Qingdao, Guangzhou) and the global (Detroit, Istanbul, Mumbai, Seoul, Beirut) to argue for the systematic erosion of physical urban space by the attention economy. Drawing on Harvey's (2003) theory of accumulation by dispossession and Zuboff's (2019) framework of surveillance capitalism, the article maintains that the rise of digital capital does not liberate space but rather dispossesses it in a new form.
I.
一个承诺，一片空地
A Promise, and an Empty Lot
Fig. 1  Google Downtown West 官方宣传页面（2021）——"一个受圣何塞启发的地方"。Source: Google / Downtown West
2021年，谷歌以这句话开始了它对圣何塞市中心最大规模城市介入的叙事："A place inspired by San José"——一个受圣何塞启发的地方。这不只是一句广告语，而是一种空间权力的宣告：私人资本有权用一座城市的历史和文化为自己的地产项目背书，然后以此作为获取公共土地的理由。
In 2021, Google opened its narrative of the largest private urban intervention in downtown San José's history with these words: "A place inspired by San José." This was not merely an advertising slogan but a declaration of spatial power: that private capital has the right to invoke a city's history and culture as endorsement for its own real estate project, and to use that endorsement as justification for acquiring public land.
Fig. 2  Downtown West 项目承诺数据：730万平方英尺办公、4000套住宅、15英亩公园、50万平方英尺零售文化空间。Source: Google / Downtown West
承诺是具体的：730万平方英尺办公空间、4000套住宅（研究中提及5900套的可能性）、15英亩公园与广场、50万平方英尺零售文化艺术酒店空间，以及对圣何塞经济的190亿美元拉动。谷歌从市政府手中以超过1.1亿美元购入约80英亩土地，承诺的条款包括：2亿美元的社区福利基金、硅谷1.5万套保障性住房，以及将圣何塞 Diridon Station 打造为连接BART轻轨的交通与城市中心。
The promises were specific: 7.3 million square feet of office space, 4,000 housing units (with a study of up to 5,900), 15 acres of parks and plazas, 500,000 square feet of retail, cultural, arts, and hotel space, and a projected $19 billion in economic impact for San José. Google purchased approximately 80 acres from the city for over $110 million. The terms included a $200 million community benefits package, 15,000 affordable housing units across Silicon Valley, and the transformation of Diridon Station into a transit and urban hub connecting BART.
Fig. 3  Downtown West 场地边界图，覆盖 Diridon Station 至 Guadalupe River 沿岸约80英亩核心地带
Fig. 5  Downtown West 鸟瞰效果图——从未实现的城市愿景。Source: Google / STUDIOS Architecture
Fig. 4  Google 的"场所原则"图示：社区、创新、自然、经济的四圈交叠。这份价值观宣言在项目搁置后成了一份空洞的文件。
然后，项目停了。2023年，随着科技股暴跌、广告收入萎缩、混合办公重塑劳动力需求，谷歌解散了开发团队，无限期暂停施工。留下的，是一片已被清除历史建筑的空地——Patty's Inn 这家运营近90年的酒吧已经消失，Poor House Bistro 被迫迁址后生意一落千丈——以及那张四圈交叠的"场所原则"图示，如今读来像是一份空洞的文件（CNBC, 2023; KQED, 2026）。
Then the project stopped. In 2023, as tech stocks plummeted, advertising revenues contracted, and hybrid work reshaped labour demand, Google disbanded its development team and suspended construction indefinitely. What remained was a cleared lot where historic buildings once stood—Patty's Inn, which had operated for nearly ninety years, was gone; Poor House Bistro had been forced to relocate and never recovered its business—alongside that four-circle diagram of "principles of place," which now reads as a hollow document (CNBC, 2023; KQED, 2026).
Fig. 6  圣何塞城市背景图：Downtown West 地块（橙色）位于城市核心地带，毗邻 SAP Center、Diridon Station 与 Guadalupe River。这不是城市边缘，而是城市的心脏。
Fig. 7 · Silicon Valley Office Vacancy Rate 硅谷写字楼空置率 Source: Joint Venture Silicon Valley, 2025 Q1
2025年Q1硅谷写字楼空置率达21.7%，持平互联网泡沫破裂后的历史峰值。这是谷歌撤退的宏观背景，也是"数字资本承诺城市，然后撤离"这一逻辑的量化注脚。
Fig. 8 · U.S. Interest Rates 美国基准利率 Source: CRFB / U.S. Department of Treasury
2022年后美联储快速加息至5.5%，建设融资成本急剧上升——这是谷歌暂停 Downtown West 的直接宏观诱因。资本在低利率时代许下的城市乌托邦承诺，在加息周期中轰然坍塌。
废墟，并非历史偶然的残余——它是资本撤退后留下的签名。谷歌的"场所原则"不是价值观，是一份营销文件；而被拆除的 Patty's Inn，是那份文件的真实代价。
A ruin is not the accidental residue of history—it is the signature capital leaves behind upon withdrawal. Google's "principles of place" were not values; they were a marketing document. And the demolished Patty's Inn is the real cost of that document.
§
II.
对照：伦敦国王十字的另一种结局
Counterpoint: King's Cross, London — A Different Outcome
2025年夏，谷歌在伦敦国王十字的新总部大楼"Platform 37"即将开放——比最初计划晚了整整十年，耗资约10亿英镑，由 Heatherwick Studio 与 Bjarke Ingels Group 联合设计，330米长、11层、100万平方英尺，屋顶设有跑道与花园，底层向公众开放。这幢建筑将容纳约4500名谷歌员工，与毗邻的6 Pancras Square 和第三栋楼合并为可容纳7000人的伦敦校园（Building Magazine, 2025; Gardiner & Theobald, 2025）。
In the summer of 2025, Google's new London headquarters "Platform 37" at King's Cross is due to open—a full decade behind its original schedule, at a cost of approximately £1 billion, designed jointly by Heatherwick Studio and Bjarke Ingels Group: 330 metres long, eleven storeys, one million square feet, with a rooftop running track and gardens, a publicly accessible ground floor. The building will house approximately 4,500 Google employees and, together with the adjacent 6 Pancras Square and a third building, will form a London campus accommodating up to 7,000 (Building Magazine, 2025; Gardiner & Theobald, 2025).
表面上，这是圣何塞的成功版本。但深看之下，国王十字的故事同样揭示出一个关于公共空间私有化的深层逻辑。开发商 Argent 主导的67英亩再开发项目中，Granary Square 等广场在法律上属于私有，适用私人规则——这正是英国城市研究者 Anna Minton（2017）所批判的 POPS（Privately Owned Public Space）现象：外观是公共的，产权是私有的，使用权是有条件的。当地居民报告称"穿着不对就感觉被盯着看"，周边的 Somers Town 社区至今没有获得穿越铁轨直达开发区的桥梁（Placetest, 2019; Edwards, 2022）。
On the surface, this appears to be the successful version of San José. But examined closely, the King's Cross story equally reveals a deep logic of public space privatisation. In the 67-acre redevelopment led by developer Argent, squares like Granary Square are legally private, governed by private rules—this is precisely the POPS (Privately Owned Public Space) phenomenon that urban researcher Anna Minton (2017) has criticised: the appearance is public, the title is private, and the right of use is conditional. Local residents have reported feeling "watched if you're not wearing the right things"; the adjacent Somers Town community still lacks a bridge across the tracks to the development (Placetest, 2019; Edwards, 2022).
Case Comparison · 案例对比 · San José vs. London King's Cross
圣何塞 Downtown West

✗ 历史建筑已被拆除，场地变为空地
✗ 承诺的社区福利未兑现
✗ 开发团队解散，重启时间表不明
✗ 本地商户因搬迁而永久损失
✗ 公共土地以优惠价出售后无法收回

Harvey（2003）：资本过度积累→城市土地投机→热潮消退→废墟与债务
London King's Cross

✓ 建设完成，谷歌总部已落成
✓ 历史建筑（煤气罐、仓库）修缮保留
~ 仅1,700套住宅，可负担比例有限
✗ POPS：广场私有，进入规则私定
✗ 周边 Somers Town 社区连接仍薄弱
✗ 绅士化已扩散至毗邻街区

Edwards (2022): "class transformation of London, trampling on the needs of low- and middle-income people to produce a zone of luxury"
两者的差异是真实的：一个建成了，一个没有。但这种差异掩盖了一个更深的共同点：无论建成与否，资本对城市空间的介入都服从同一套逻辑——空间的价值由资本定义，社区的命运由资本决定，风险由居民承担，收益由股东持有。国王十字"建成了"，但那里的原住居民能以什么价格租到什么样的房子？圣何塞"失败了"，但谷歌持有的那80英亩土地仍然是谷歌的资产。两种结局都没有改变权力的结构。
The difference between them is real: one was built, one was not. But this difference conceals a deeper commonality: whether or not capital completes its intervention in urban space, the same logic applies—the value of space is defined by capital, the fate of communities is decided by capital, risks are borne by residents, and returns accrue to shareholders. King's Cross "succeeded," but at what rents can the original residents access what quality of housing? San José "failed," but the eighty acres Google owns remain Google's asset. Neither outcome changes the structure of power.
§
III.
中国的变奏：上海、天津、青岛、广州
Chinese Variations: Shanghai, Tianjin, Qingdao, Guangzhou
如果说圣何塞和伦敦是数字资本与实体空间关系的西方前沿，那么中国提供了一组在更短时间内、更大规模上完成的变奏曲。中国的城市更新从1990年代末开始加速，其核心逻辑与西方的绅士化并无本质区别——只是速度更快、规模更大、国家角色更直接。
If San José and London represent the Western frontier of the relationship between digital capital and physical space, China offers a set of variations completed at greater speed and on a larger scale. China's urban renewal accelerated from the late 1990s; its core logic does not fundamentally differ from Western gentrification—it is simply faster, larger in scale, and involves a more direct state role.
城市 · City
案例 · Case
中文描述
English Description
上海 Shanghai
新天地石库门 / Xintiandi
1996年，卢湾区政府与瑞安集团合作，投资14亿将太平桥石库门街区改造为新天地——保留外立面，拆除95%的内部空间，全体居民迁出，改为高档餐饮商业区。其成功在于商业运作，而非历史保护：文化价值被转化为地产溢价，场所记忆被转化为消费符号（孙彤宇，同济大学；Archina, 2019）。毗邻的田子坊则走了截然不同的路——居民保留产权，自下而上改造，但随后也遭遇过度商业化的蚕食。
In 1996, Luwan District government partnered with Shui On Group to invest ¥1.4 billion renovating the Taipingqiao shikumen district into Xintiandi—preserving facades, demolishing 95% of interiors, relocating all residents, and converting the area to upmarket dining and retail. Its success was commercial, not preservationist: cultural value was converted to real estate premium, and spatial memory to consumable symbol. The adjacent Tianzifang took a different path—residents retained property rights in a bottom-up renovation—but it too subsequently suffered from over-commercialisation.
天津 Tianjin
五大道 / Five Avenues
五大道拥有逾2000栋风格各异的近代建筑，是梁启超、张学良等历史人物的故居所在。2011年被列为历史文化街区，2014年获评4A景区，此后原住民迁出，商业资本涌入，历史肌理在网红滤镜下变得既真实又虚假。这是我生长的地方。那种属于一个街区的生活气息的消散，比任何一栋建筑的拆除都更安静、也更彻底（Xiang et al., 2023; Zhang, 2019）。
The Five Avenues contains over 2,000 villas in diverse architectural styles and was home to historical figures including Liang Qichao and Zhang Xueliang. Designated a historic and cultural street in 2011 and a 4A tourist area in 2014, it subsequently saw original residents displaced and commercial capital move in; historical fabric became simultaneously real and simulated beneath influencer photography filters. This is where I grew up. The dissolution of that ineffable quality of life belonging to a neighbourhood was quieter and more complete than any demolition (Xiang et al., 2023; Zhang, 2019).
青岛 Qingdao
八大关 / Eight Passes
德租界留下的八大关历史街区，以欧式建筑与梧桐树道著称，近年来同样经历从居住街区向旅游消费区的转型。原住民迁出后，大量民宿、网红咖啡馆涌入，历史建筑被改造为消费场景。与五大道相似：空间的物理外壳被保留，社会肌理被置换。"青岛文艺"成为流量标签，而不再是一种生活方式。
The Eight Passes historic district, a legacy of the German concession period known for its European architecture and plane-tree boulevards, has similarly undergone transformation from a residential neighbourhood to a tourism consumption zone. As original residents left, guesthouses and influencer cafés proliferated, with historic buildings converted into consumption settings. As with the Five Avenues: the physical shell of space is preserved; the social fabric is replaced. "Qingdao aesthetics" has become a traffic label rather than a way of life.
广州 Guangzhou
永庆坊 / Yongqing Fang
广州荔湾区的永庆坊以骑楼和西关大屋为特色，近年被改造为网红打卡地，引入轻奢品牌和文创工作室。李小龙祖居在此，官方叙事将其包装为"活化历史"的样板。然而批评者指出：原住民同样经历了被动迁移，历史街区的商业化程度已与其所标榜的"社区活化"背道而驰。广州的案例尤其说明了"活化"话语与实际驱逐之间的修辞鸿沟。
Yongqing Fang in Guangzhou's Liwan District, characterised by arcade buildings and Xiguan mansions, has been transformed into an influencer destination with upscale brands and creative studios. Bruce Lee's ancestral home is here; official narrative packages the development as a model of "activating history." Critics note, however, that original residents similarly experienced displacement, and the commercialisation of the historic district has moved in the opposite direction from its proclaimed "community activation." The Guangzhou case particularly illustrates the rhetorical gap between "revitalisation" discourse and actual displacement.
这四个中国案例揭示出一个共同的结构：历史街区被纳入文化资本逻辑，外立面作为符号被保留，内部社会关系被清除，原住民被以"改善居住条件"为名迁出，资本以"文化保护"为名进入。这与 Banks（2023）描述的"城市真实性政治"如出一辙：废墟的美感（或历史的质感）被资本化，然后摧毁产生这种美感的社会条件本身。中国的特殊性在于：这一过程往往由国家和资本共谋完成，速度更快，阻力更小，也因此更难被命名为"剥夺"。
These four Chinese cases reveal a shared structure: historic districts are incorporated into the logic of cultural capital; facades are preserved as signs; internal social relations are cleared; original residents are relocated under the name of "improving living conditions"; capital enters under the name of "cultural preservation." This mirrors precisely what Banks (2023) describes as the politics of the "city authentic": the aesthetic of decay (or the texture of history) is capitalised upon, then the social conditions that produced it are destroyed. What is distinctive about China is that this process is typically completed through complicity between state and capital, more rapidly, with less resistance—and therefore harder to name as "dispossession."
§
IV.
全球谱系：废墟经济学的世界地图
A Global Typology: The World Map of Ruin Economics
从圣何塞到伦敦，从上海到天津，这一机制并非地方性的偶发，而是全球资本主义城市化的系统性结构。Hackworth 与 Smith（2001）将这一过程描述为绅士化的"波次"演进：从1960年代国家主导的零散再投资，到1980年代私人资本主导的全球化扩散，到1990年代后跨国资本与新自由主义政策的结合，到今天的平台资本主义介入。Aalbers（2019）进一步指出，第五波绅士化的核心已经是住房金融化与平台资本的合谋——这是注意力经济与实体空间剥夺之间的结构性连接点。
From San José to London, from Shanghai to Tianjin, this mechanism is not a local anomaly but a systemic structure of global capitalist urbanisation. Hackworth and Smith (2001) describe this process as the "wave" evolution of gentrification: from the state-led sporadic reinvestment of the 1960s, to the globalised expansion of private capital in the 1980s, to the combination of transnational capital and neoliberal policy after the 1990s, to today's intervention of platform capitalism. Aalbers (2019) further argues that the core of fifth-wave gentrification is the complicity between housing financialisation and platform capital—precisely the structural connection between the attention economy and physical space dispossession.
城市 · City
中文
English
底特律 Detroit
去工业化 + 数字复兴叙事
汽车工业撤离制造大规模废墟；科技资本以"城市复兴"之名进入，重建仅惠及白人专业阶层，原住黑人社区被系统性驱逐。铁锈的美感被 Instagram 化，废墟成为消费符号（Harvey, 2012）。
Deindustrialisation created mass abandonment; tech capital entered under "urban renaissance," rebuilding in ways that benefited white professionals while systematically displacing the original Black community. The aesthetic of rust became Instagrammable; ruins became consumable signs.
伊斯坦布尔 Istanbul
帝国废墟 + 旅游凝视
奥斯曼帝国的衰落留下了 Pamuk（2003）所称的 hüzün——集体性忧郁，根植于博斯普鲁斯沿岸的木质老宅与失落的文明记忆。这种忧郁本身被旅游经济消费：真实的颓败被包装为奇观，居民在承载记忆的空间中反而成为异乡人。
The Ottoman collapse left what Pamuk (2003) calls hüzün—collective melancholy rooted in Bosphorus yalis and lost civilisational memory. This melancholy is itself consumed by tourism: authentic decay is packaged as spectacle, and residents become strangers in spaces carrying their own memories.
孟买 Mumbai
纺织工人罢工 + 高端地产
1982年纺织工人大罢工失败后，孟买棉纺厂区去工业化，数百英亩土地被高端地产吞没。制造业在城市 GDP 中的占比从1993年的39%降至2010年的25%，而土地价格飙升至全球最高水平之一（Banerjee-Guha, 2002）。
Following the defeat of the 1982 textile workers' strike, Mumbai's mill districts deindustrialised, with hundreds of acres consumed by luxury development. Manufacturing's share of city GDP fell from 39% (1993) to 25% (2010), while land prices rose to among the world's highest.
首尔江南 Seoul
压缩式绅士化 + 平台经济
韩国在数十年内完成了西方百年的城市化进程，产生了 Shin（2016）所称的"压缩性绅士化"：资本积累速度超过文化认同的形成速度，历史街区在被保护之前已先行消失。Kakao、Naver 等平台重构城市体验，线下空间的价值越来越取决于数字平台上的可见度。
South Korea completed in decades what the West took a century to undergo, producing what Shin (2016) calls "compressed gentrification": historic neighbourhoods disappeared before they could be protected. Platform economies (Kakao, Naver) restructured urban experience; offline space value increasingly depends on digital platform visibility.
贝鲁特 Beirut
战后重建 + 资本独占
内战后，哈里里主导成立 Solidere 公司，以"重建"为名将贝鲁特市中心私有化。数千名原住民被驱逐，战前多元文化街区被统一风格精品区取代。Solidere 至今持有大量土地，使贝鲁特市中心成为全球最典型的私有化历史城区（Schmid, 2006）。
After the civil war, Solidere company privatised downtown Beirut under the banner of "reconstruction." Thousands of original residents were displaced; the prewar multicultural neighbourhood was replaced by a uniformly styled boutique district. Solidere still holds extensive landholdings, making central Beirut a paradigm of privatised historic urban core.
这份名单还可以继续延伸：约翰内斯堡的 Hillbrow、那不勒斯的 Scampia、里约热内卢的法维拉——每个案例的驱动因素不尽相同，但结构逻辑惊人地一致。Harvey（2003）称之为剥夺性积累的"空间修复"（spatial fix）：资本通过开拓新的地理空间来解决过度积累危机。当实体城市空间被充分开拓，资本便转向下一个领域。我们正处于这个转折点上。
The list extends further: Johannesburg's Hillbrow, Naples' Scampia, Rio de Janeiro's favelas—each driven by different proximate factors yet following a structural logic of striking consistency. Harvey (2003) calls this the "spatial fix" of accumulation by dispossession: capital resolves its overaccumulation crises by opening up new geographical spaces. When physical urban space is sufficiently exhausted, capital turns to the next domain. We stand at that turning point.
Solidere 广场有自己的 Instagram 账号；Hillbrow 的路灯坏了三年没人修。这不是偶然的对比，而是同一套逻辑的两端——资本决定哪些空间"值得"被数字记录，进而决定哪些文明"值得"被保存。
Solidere plaza has its own Instagram account; Hillbrow's streetlights have been broken for three years. This is not an incidental contrast but the two ends of a single logic—capital determines which spaces are "worth" digital documentation, and thereby which civilisations are "worth" preservation.
§
V.
废墟不会说谎，但战场正在转移
Ruins Do Not Lie — But the Battlefield Is Shifting
废墟是诚实的。圣何塞的空地裸露着被拆除的历史建筑的地基；天津五大道修缮外立面的背后是换了一拨又一拨人的内部空间；贝鲁特 Solidere 区域里没有原住民的声音；底特律黑人社区的创伤不出现在任何品牌的"城市复兴"叙事里。它们都在以沉默的方式陈述同一件事：资本曾经来过，然后走了，留下的是别人的损失。
Ruins are honest. The cleared lot in San José exposes the foundations of demolished historic buildings; behind the restored facades of Tianjin's Five Avenues lies interior space that has cycled through one occupant after another; Beirut's Solidere district contains no voice of original residents; the trauma of Detroit's Black communities appears nowhere in any brand's "urban renaissance" narrative. All of them are making the same statement in silence: capital was here, and then it left, and what remained was someone else's loss.
然而，这场关于实体空间剥夺的故事，其战场正在发生一个深刻的转移。注意力经济的逻辑曾经是：在实体空间上叠加一层数字表征，改变空间的可见性与价值分配——社交媒体的凝视决定哪条街道值得投资，哪栋建筑值得保留。这是一种叠加关系。但游戏经济、VR/AR、具身 AI——这些正在建造的，不再是实体空间之上的数字层，而是一套与实体空间竞争的替代性空间体系。
Yet the battlefield of this story of physical space dispossession is undergoing a profound shift. The logic of the attention economy once operated as a digital representation layered atop physical space, altering its visibility and value distribution—social media's gaze determining which streets are worth investing in, which buildings worth preserving. This was a relation of overlay. But gaming economies, VR/AR, embodied AI—what these are constructing is no longer a digital layer above physical space, but a rival spatial system competing with physical space itself.
这是一个更大的问题，需要一套新的分析框架。Harvey（2003）称实体空间的开拓是资本的"空间修复"——当一个地理空间被充分榨取，资本便转向下一个。我们现在看到的，可能是人类历史上第一次真正意义上的"本体论修复"（ontological fix）：资本不再转向新的地理空间，而是转向现实本身的新维度。废墟诚实，但关于废墟的问题还没有结束——它只是换了一个战场。
This is a larger problem requiring a new analytical framework. Harvey (2003) describes the opening of physical space as capital's "spatial fix"—when one geographical space is sufficiently exhausted, capital moves to the next. What we may be witnessing now is the first genuinely "ontological fix" in human history: capital no longer turns to new geographical spaces but to new dimensions of reality itself. Ruins are honest—but the questions that ruins pose are not yet closed. The battlefield has simply shifted.
下一篇 · Next in Series
空间失落的历史谱系：从后殖民废墟到虚拟空间的积累逻辑
A Historical Genealogy of Spatial Loss: From Postcolonial Ruins to the Accumulation Logic of Virtual Space
如果废墟经济学是一种全球性的、当下的机制，那么第三篇要追问的是：这一机制的历史从哪里开始？从战后去殖民化城市的空间断裂，到英美去工业化的废墟地带，到注意力经济的符号消费，到正在兴起的虚拟空间积累——这是一个连续的剥夺逻辑，还是在每个阶段都发生了质的突变？而当数字资本向虚拟空间转移，被实体空间剥夺的人，是否只是在一个更不可见的层面被再次剥夺？
·
If ruin economics is a global and contemporary mechanism, the third instalment asks: where does this mechanism's history begin? From the spatial ruptures of postwar decolonising cities, to the ruin-scapes of Anglo-American deindustrialisation, to the symbolic consumption of the attention economy, to the emerging accumulation of virtual space—is this a continuous logic of dispossession, or does each phase represent a qualitative mutation? And as digital capital shifts toward virtual space, are those already dispossessed of physical space simply being dispossessed again, at a less visible register?
